methods.description: Process for excavating archaeological sites and collecting samples, specifically at Daisy Cave, using hand tools and screening methods to preserve artifacts and stratigraphy.

steps.description: Excavation was performed by hand trowel and finer methods (e.g., straws, dental picks, and small brushes), following natural stratigraphic layers.
steps.searchable_summary: perform manual excavation using hand tools following stratigraphic layers

steps.description: Using a combination of 1/8 and 1/16-inch mesh to screen sediments in the field.
All materials were transported to the University of Oregon for analysis, where they were rinsed in tap water and subsequently stored in a climate-controlled laboratory.
steps.searchable_summary: transport and clean samples, then store in controlled conditions

methods.method_name: Radiocarbon Dating and Stable Isotope Analysis
methods.description: Procedures for processing bone samples for radiocarbon dating and stable isotope analysis to determine dietary sources and chronological data.

steps.description: The metapodial was processed at the Penn State Human Paleoecology and Isotope Geochemistry Laboratory.
steps.searchable_summary: process bone sample for isotope analysis

steps.description: The gelatin was ultrafiltered to a > 30 kDa fraction with a yield of 6.9%.
steps.searchable_summary: ultrafilter gelatin to obtain > 30 kDa fraction

steps.description: An aliquot of 2.20 mg of collagen was subsequently combusted and sent to UCIAMS as CO2 for graphitization and AMS analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: combust collagen and prepare for AMS analysis

steps.description: 0.73 mg of collagen was sent to Yale for analyses of %C, %N, δ13C and δ15N.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze collagen for carbon and nitrogen isotopes

steps.description: We used the R package simmr to estimate the proportional contributions of terrestrial and marine food sources to the value of isotopes.
steps.searchable_summary: use simmr package to model dietary contributions
